mission.title: kubevirt: download progress for image quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.31 while building
mission.description: download progress for image quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.31 while building. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubevirt deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubevirt version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
helm list -n kubevirt 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevirt installation.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
hello, when I run `make cluster-up`, it downloads the `quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.31` image.

steps.title: Apply the fix for download progress for image quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.31…
steps.description:
Hi all, I am interested in working on this.  I see the following options for this:

1. Copy line-by-line the output of the `cli.ImagePull` function to stdout, however this is going to be a lot of lines, as the image `quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.3.1:2504071522-2880e252` is about 17GB large. The good
```yaml
time="2025-03-14T14:00:38Z" level=info msg="Using remote image quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.31:2503070845-e6c41d6e"
Downloading ...............................................................................................................
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "download progress for image quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.31…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi all, I am interested in working on this.  I see the following options for this:

1. Copy line-by-line the output of the `cli.ImagePull` function to stdout, however this is going to be a lot of lines, as the image `quay.io/kubevirtci/k8s-1.3.1:2504071522-2880e252` is about 17GB large. The good thing here is that the progress bars can be printed, but there are lots of lines in stdout.
2.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
